question: A .NET team plans to integrate with Zeebe using `zeebe-client-csharp` from the Camunda Community Hub. Per the community-supported clients docs, what is the support status of this library?

options.text: It is maintained by the community and is not part of the commercial Camunda product; Camunda does not support community extensions as part of its commercial services to enterprise customers.
It is one of the core Camunda-maintained clients and is supported on equal footing with the TypeScript SDK.
It is officially supported by Camunda only when the cluster is on the Enterprise tier.
It is deprecated; the docs direct .NET users to the Camunda Spring Boot Starter for integration.

a.text: The page's opening note says Camunda extensions found in the Camunda Community Hub are maintained by the community and are not part of the commercial Camunda product, and that Camunda does not support community extensions as part of its commercial services to enterprise customers.

b.text: The page distinguishes core Camunda-maintained clients (the TypeScript SDK and the Camunda Spring Boot Starter) from these community-maintained libraries, including `zeebe-client-csharp`.

c.text: No license tier upgrades the support status of community clients — Camunda explicitly declines to support them commercially.

d.text: The Spring Boot Starter is a Java library; the docs do not redirect .NET users to it and do not mark the C# client as deprecated.

explanation: Camunda officially supports the TypeScript SDK and the Camunda Spring Boot Starter. Everything else listed under Community Hub — including the C#, Go, Python, Rust, Quarkus, and Ruby clients — is community-maintained and explicitly excluded from Camunda's commercial support. Teams should evaluate each before adopting.
